title: Crunchy Filipino Swede Stir-fry
description: This sick-day-friendly dish combines sweet and crunchy flavors with swede, a nutritious root vegetable that's perfect for the cooler months.

ingredients.name: Swede
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with another root vegetable like carrots if needed.

instructions: Trim the swede and peel it. Cut into small cubes.
Chop the onion into fine pieces, finely mince the garlic cloves.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add minced garlic to the pan, stirring frequently for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add swede cubes to the pan. Season with salt and cook until slightly softened but still crisp, around 5-7 minutes.
If using fish sauce, add it now and stir well. If not, just mix in soy sauce.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until all ingredients are evenly cooked and the swede is tender yet crispy.
